experiences.intentions: Two boxes: (2,1) is directly below target (1,1), and player at (1,2) can get to the right of that box by moving around through the open area, then push it left? Better: to place box (2,1) onto target (1,1), it must be pushed up from (2,1), requiring player at (3,1). That square is reachable by going down from current position through open cells to (3,1). This is the safest first objective because target is adjacent and avoids corner deadlock. Immediate best step is to start moving toward (3,1) by going down.

experiences.intentions: There are two boxes: one already adjacent to a target at (2,1) below target (1,1), and another at (3,4) that likely can later be routed to target (4,2). The immediate best play is to solve the left box safely by pushing it up onto the target. This is not a deadlock because the destination is a target. The player is at (2,2), directly right of the box at (2,1), so a push left is legal: box moves from (2,1) to (2,0)? Wait, that's a wall, so not legal. To push the box at (2,1) up, I need to stand below it at (3,1), but from current board (3,1) is floor and reachable by moving down then left around? First move should be down? However recent history already did down, but current board still shows player at (2,2), so use current state only. From (2,2), moving down to (3,2) is blocked by wall. Moving left would contact the box but cannot push into wall. Moving up to (1,2) is free and is the first step toward reaching below/around the left box via the open top corridor? Actually target row has wall at (1,4) only; from (1,2) can go left to (1,1) target, but cannot get below box directly. Better plan: reposition through lower area to get to (3,1), but from (2,2) the only open moves are up and right. Right opens access to the right chamber and then down/around back left through row 5 to approach box (3,4) or eventually left side. Since immediate target box cannot be pushed now, best is to start repositioning without oscillation. Move right to open central corridor and keep options for solving the right box or circling to lower-left approach.

experiences.intentions: The remaining box is at (5,5) on the bottom row just above the wall boundary, and the target is at (4,2). Since the box cannot be pushed down or right due to walls, the only useful first push will eventually be left, but I am currently at (4,4), which is above-left of the box. To push the box left, I must stand on its right at (5,6), impossible because that's a wall. So instead I need to move to the left of the box and push it up? Check: pushing up from (6,5) is impossible because bottom wall. Pushing left from right side impossible. Pushing right from left side would shove it into the bottom-right corner at (5,6), a deadlock. Therefore the box position at (5,5) means the only feasible push is from above: if I stand at (4,5), I can push it down, but that sends it into the corner/wall at (6,5), impossible. Re-evaluate coordinates: box at (5,5) can be pushed left if player stands at (5,6), blocked; pushed up if player stands at (6,5), blocked; pushed right if player stands at (5,4), but destination (5,6) is floor against wall and likely dead; pushed down if player stands at (4,5), but destination (6,5) is wall. So no productive push exists from current box placement. Best immediate action is to move right to (4,5) to inspect adjacency and avoid oscillating near center, while not making a deadlocking push.

experiences.intentions: The remaining box is at (5,5) adjacent to the right wall and bottom wall below, so pushing it right or down is impossible and dangerous. The target is at (4,2), so the box ultimately needs to move left across row 5, then up near column 2. To push the box left, the player must stand at (5,6), which is a wall, so left pushes are impossible while the box stays on the right wall. Therefore the first priority is to move the box off the wall by pushing it up from (4,5) into (4,5). The player is currently directly above the box at (4,5), and the square beyond the box upward is floor at (4,5), so an immediate upward push is legal and best. This stops oscillation and makes future left pushes possible from the right side.

experiences.intentions: The only unsolved box is at (5,5), adjacent to the right wall. The target is at (4,2), so the box must eventually move left across row 5 and then up near column 2. Immediate danger is pushing the box further into a bad wall/corner situation; pushing right is impossible and pushing down would hit the bottom wall. To push the box left, I need to stand to its right at (5,6), which is a wall, so that is impossible. Therefore the first necessary step is to move the box up from (5,5) to (4,5), because from there it can later be pushed left along row 4 toward the target. The player is already directly above the box at (4,4), so moving down would only step next to it, while a push down is invalid. From (4,4), the player is left of the box only if the box were at (4,5), which it is not. The correct immediate legal push is to get below/above alignment? Since the box is below-right at (5,5), first I should move down to (5,4) to stand left of the box and prepare to push it right? But pushing right would be impossible because of wall at (5,6). Better is to route around so I can stand above the box and push it up; currently I am at (4,4), not above the box. I need to move right to (4,5), but that cell is above the box and is empty, letting me set up a push down? Wait, target plan requires box up, so I need to stand below box at (6,5), impossible due wall. Therefore box cannot be pushed up from current location. Since left push is impossible due no standing space on right, and up push is impossible due no standing space below, the only sensible immediate reposition is right to (4,5), which may reveal a better path around the box without oscillating vertically.

From the current player position (4,5), I am directly above the box, so I can immediately push it left by standing to its right? No—pushing left requires being to the right of the box, impossible due to the wall. Pushing down is impossible due to the bottom wall. The only productive immediate push is push up from above? Actually player is above the box, so push down would try to move box to (6,5), blocked. Therefore I should reposition to the left of the box so I can eventually push it left off the wall. The safe first move is left to go around through the open space and stop oscillating.

Since the box is on the right wall, pushing it further right or down would deadlock, especially into the bottom-right corner. To solve, I need first to push the box up from (5,5) to (4,5), which requires the player to stand below it at (6,5) — impossible because row 6 is a wall. So instead I must move the box left along row 5, but that requires standing to its right at (5,6), also impossible because col 6 is a wall. Therefore the box cannot be pushed from its current square by direct adjacency on those sides. The best immediate action is to reposition toward the box’s accessible side and avoid repeating invalid left pushes; moving down gets closer to the only open adjacent cell at (5,4), from which future routing can be reassessed.

So first reposition toward the open area to approach future vertical movement after checking access; moving right to (4,5) is the most useful adjacent setup and avoids oscillating away from the box.

experiences.intentions: The box is at (5,5) with wall at (5,6), so pushing right is impossible and risky. Target is at (4,2). Best route is to move the box left along row 5, then up near column 2. To push left, the player must stand to the right of the box, but the box is currently against the right wall so that is impossible now. Therefore first reposition the player to push the box upward from below? Also impossible because below is wall at row 6. The only legal productive push on the box from current layout is push up from player at (5,4)? No, player is left of the box, so push right would hit wall. Since immediate pushes are bad/impossible, reposition. Moving up opens a route around through row 4 to later approach the box from above and then potentially push it left after changing its position. Avoid oscillating left-right; choose a fresh repositioning move.

experiences.intentions: There is one unsolved box at (5,5) and one target at (4,2). The box is currently against the right wall, so pushing it further right is impossible and pushing it down would deadlock against the bottom wall. The safe immediate plan is to move the box left along row 5, then later push it up near column 2 onto the target row. To push the box left, the player must stand to the right of the box, at (5,6), but that is a wall, so left-push is impossible from the current box position. The only productive first push on the box is push up from (4,5), sending the box from (5,5) to (4,5). The player is currently at (4,4), immediately left of the box's current column and can step right to (4,5), which is empty floor, to get into position above the box for the next move. This avoids oscillation and prepares a necessary safe push.

From the current layout, the box cannot be pushed right or down due to walls, so it must eventually be pushed left across row 5, then up toward row 4, then left onto the target. To start that plan, I need to get above the box at (4,5), which is exactly the player's current position, so the immediate productive move is to push the box down from above into (5,5)->(6,5) only if legal. But row 6 is a wall, so push down is illegal. From current position adjacent above the box, the better reposition is to move left to circle around and approach the box from the right side later, avoiding oscillation and not trapping it against the wall further. Moving left keeps flexibility and begins the route around the central obstacle toward the box's leftward path.

To push down, player must be at (4,5), which is reachable by moving right first. That would push the box into bottom wall at (6,5), impossible. Therefore from current geometry the box at (5,5) is actually immovable: right wall blocks pushing left setup, bottom wall blocks pushing up setup, and destination cells for right/down are walls. Since no push is possible now or after one legal move, choose a non-oscillating repositioning move that explores access while staying safe; moving left begins circling through open space toward the target side rather than repeating the recent right/left oscillation near current spot.

To get it to the target, the first useful push is upward from (5,5) to (4,5), because from there it can later be moved left across open floor toward column 2 and then down/up as needed. Better is to reposition first by moving up to get around the box so we can eventually stand below it and push it up. From (5,4), moving up to (4,4) is legal and starts that route without oscillating.

experiences.intentions: The only unsolved box is at (5,5) with target at (4,2). The box is currently adjacent to the right wall, so pushing right is impossible and risky. To move it toward the target, we ultimately need to push it left across row 5 and then up toward row 4. From the current position, the player is immediately left of the box at (5,4), and the space beyond the box to the left is the player’s current side while the box’s right side is wall, so push left is not legal from here. The best immediate step is to reposition upward into the open area so I can route around and approach the box from the right side or set up a safer plan, avoiding the recent oscillation and failed pushes.

experiences.intentions: The only unsolved box is at (5,5) and the target is at (4,2). From the current layout, the box cannot go further right or down because of walls, and pushing it down/right would deadlock. The viable plan is to move the box left across row 5, then later push it up toward row 4 and left onto the target. To push the box left, I need to stand immediately to its right at (5,6), but that is a wall, so left pushes are impossible from here. To push it up from below is also impossible because (6,5) is a wall. Therefore the immediate priority is to reposition to the left/above side of the box so I can eventually push it leftward from a different corridor if possible; from the current player position (4,4), moving down to (5,4) is legal and gets adjacent to the box without risking an accidental deadlock. This also stops the recent oscillation and sets up a potential push right/left reassessment from row 5.

To reach the target, the box ultimately needs to move left across row 5 and then up near column 2. The player is at (5,4), immediately left of the box, and the cell to the right of the box (5,6) is a wall, so push right is impossible. Push left is legal because destination (5,4) is floor, but recent history shows repeated push-left attempts likely from different states and oscillation afterward. Still, from the current exact state, the productive first step is to push the box left away from the wall, reducing deadlock risk and starting its path toward column 2. Moving first would just delay the necessary push and down is blocked by wall. So target this box and begin by shifting it left.

experiences.intentions: The remaining box is at (5,5) and target is at (4,2). The box is against the right wall, so pushing it further right is impossible and pushing it down would deadlock against the bottom wall. Best plan is to move the box left along row 5, but first I must get to the right side of the box; that is impossible because wall bounds the right edge. Therefore the first necessary push is upward from (4,5), moving the box from (5,5) to (4,5), which frees it from the bottom row and allows later leftward pushes toward the target. The player is currently at (4,4), immediately left of the square (4,5), so stepping right to (4,5) is impossible because that square is empty but then I would not be below/above the box. To push up, I need to stand below the box at (6,5), impossible due to wall. Instead, from current position adjacent above-left, I can move down to (5,4) to stand left of the box and then push right is impossible due wall. The only productive immediate repositioning is right to (4,5), placing myself above the box so next move can be push down? But pushing down would send box into bottom wall, impossible. Still, moving right is the correct reposition toward interacting with the box and stops the up/down oscillation around (4,4)/(5,4).

experiences.intentions: The only unsolved box is at (5,5), adjacent to the right wall. The target is at (4,2), so the box must first be moved left along row 5, then eventually up toward row 4 and left to col 2. Pushing the box down or right is impossible due to walls, and pushing it up from below is impossible because row 6 is all walls, so the immediate safe and necessary plan is to get to the right of the box? Actually the player is currently at (4,4), not in position to push. To push the box left, the player must stand at (5,6), but that is a wall, so left-pushing is impossible from the right side. Therefore the box must first be pushed up from (5,5) to (4,5), which requires the player to stand below at (6,5), impossible due to wall. Check other directions: push left requires standing at (5,6) wall, push right requires standing at (5,4) with destination (5,6) wall, impossible, push down requires standing at (4,5) with destination (6,5) wall, impossible. So no push is currently legal at all. Best move is to reposition adjacent to inspect/control space near the box without oscillating; moving down from (4,4) to (5,4) is legal and sets up next to the box, possibly for future push analysis.

Since the box cannot be pushed right or down, the only productive first push is upward from below impossible, or left from its right impossible due to wall; so I must inspect access. Player at (4,4) is directly above-left of the box and can move down to (5,4), placing left of the box. From there the next critical action will be push right? No, that would be illegal into wall. The real goal is to get below/around if possible, but bottom wall blocks below, so the only feasible box movement from current geometry is push left from (5,4), moving box from (5,5) to (5,4). This also follows the reflection to stop oscillating and start the upward sequence before shifting left.

The target is at (4,2), so the box ultimately needs to go left across row 5 and then up/left into column 2. From the current player position at (5,4), the player is immediately left of the box, and the square to the right of the box at (5,6) is a wall, so pushing right is impossible. To make progress and avoid oscillation, the best immediate action is to push the box left from (5,5) to (5,4), moving it away from the wall and toward the target path.
